authority: The engine and executable guards determine admission. V36 is a named forward-forcing composition of admitted relations, not a relabelled theorem derivation.

composition.boundary_frontiers: The binary count gives exactly two linear-chain boundaries; each propagates all eight V35 contexts.
composition.candidate_grammar: Two boundaries times eight contexts generates exactly sixteen directional full-chain candidates, independently equal to the closed quartet-transition census.
composition.complete_chain_reconciliation: Every generated candidate is compared under the unchanged admitted V3 total order and deterministic state-path tie break.

required_derivation_guards.assembled_form_minimality: The generated +,-,*,/ grammar proves that neither bare forced ingredient reaches sixteen; the two-boundary/context form genuinely requires one operation.
required_derivation_guards.dependency_trace_to_the_One_or_an_engine: Binary boundaries trace to b=2; eight contexts and sixteen quartet transitions trace through admitted V35 to the V2 Protein engine closure.
required_derivation_guards.engine_halt_on_violation: Ernos and Python guards halt on boundary, context, candidate, source, import, configuration, deterministic replay or seal drift.
required_derivation_guards.generated_complete_candidate_space: Both chain directions preserve all eight contexts, producing exactly 2*8=16 directional full-chain candidates.
required_derivation_guards.measured_provenance_excluded_from_deriva: Selector input is exactly run_id and sequence; target access occurs only after complete seal verification.
required_derivation_guards.named_shape_uniqueness: Among every one-operation +,-,*,/ form over two boundaries and eight contexts, only multiplication reproduces the independently closed sixteen-transition census; the admitted V3 total order resolves the complete runtime candidate set.
required_derivation_guards.purpose_matched_real_execution: Required before runtime promotion and recorded separately after the sealed ubiquitin L76 execution.
